title: problem_card (v0 DRAFT)
description: DRAFT schema per pivot/arena_problem_atlas_sandbox_vision_2026-05-14.md Part 4 (Layer 5.5 Problem Atlas). A structured map of an unsolved problem with its hardness signature and the navigation primitives needed to support Arena/Sandbox infrastructure. NOT a contract change — sits alongside the 7 existing substrate_block schemas as a draft for Aporia review. Per session-prompt 2026-05-14 Track 4 (optional). Will be promoted to v1.0 only after Aporia adjudication and a 10-card MVP smoke test demonstrates the schema captures the navigation signal the Arena needs.
_status_note: v0 draft (currently 0.2.0; Aporia adjudicated v0.1.0 on 2026-05-15 with APPROVE + 1 schema addition (field_invariants_used) + tightened MVP smoke gate). Promotion path to v1.0.0: (1) Aporia authors 10-card MVP smoke; (2) all 6 smoke criteria pass (all 8 hardness dimensions exercised, >=1 representation_shift_witness, >=1 cross-transport candidate, >=2 conceptual_absences, all primary citations resolve, >=3 distinct domain_tags); (3) Techne lands the v1.0 wiring (drop _status field, rename problem_card_v1.json, register into validate_substrate_blocks.py + parse_substrate_blocks.py, bump _schema_version to 1.0.0); (4) coordinated commit pair from both sides. Until then, do NOT use in production substrate_block emission.

_status.description: v0 DRAFT marker. Required at v0 to make it impossible for a draft block to leak into the production substrate by accident. Promotes to absent at v1.0.0.

id.title: Problem card ID
id.description: PC-NNNN format. Allocated by Aporia at registration time. PC-XXX as placeholder when proposing.

statement.title: Problem statement
statement.description: Natural-language problem statement. Includes the precise mathematical formulation, current best bound (if any), and the canonical phrasing used in the field. Citations belong in known_attacks / candidate_representations / hardness_signature, not here.

status.title: Resolution status
status.description: open: no proof or refutation. partially-known: subset settled (e.g. specific cases proven). numerical-evidence-only: extensive computation supports but no proof. independent-of-axioms: proven independent of standard axioms (CH-style). settled: included for completeness when card describes a recently-solved problem (still useful for the Atlas to record HOW it closed).

hardness_signature.description: Required: weighted vector over the 8-dimension hardness taxonomy. Per the operating thesis (vision doc Part 4): hard problems are hard because the current language compresses them badly; this signature names the compression failure mode.

method_gap_patterns.title: Documented method-gap patterns
method_gap_patterns.description: Each entry is a structured record of 'we can prove X with exponent 0.51 but need 0.5' or 'we can prove pointwise for random but not deterministic.' Each known method-gap becomes a queryable substrate object.

representation_shift_witnesses.title: Successful coordinate-change witnesses
representation_shift_witnesses.description: Cases where shifting the representation closed the gap (numbers->ideals, curves->function fields, counting->geometry). Each witness records the before/after representations + the theorem that closed when the shift was applied. Empty list = no successful shift documented yet.

precursor_dependencies.title: Required precursor concepts/theorems
precursor_dependencies.description: Directed-graph edges: 'problem X needs concept Y or theorem Z established first.' Wiles needed modular-forms theory; modular-forms theory needed earlier scaffolding. Each entry names a required precursor + relationship type.

conceptual_absences.title: Registered missing mathematical objects
conceptual_absences.description: Explicit registration that the problem needs an object that doesn't exist yet. Substrate value-prop sharpens here: forces naming of what's missing. Each entry: the absent object's tentative name, what role it would play, what existing structure most-resembles it.

musical_signature.title: Attack history (which methods made progress, which failed)
musical_signature.description: Structured record of attempted attacks on the problem with their outcome. Two problems with similar musical signatures are candidates for cross-transport. Empty list = no documented attempts (rare; mostly applies to extremely new problems).

known_attacks.title: Catalog of canonical attack vectors documented for this problem
known_attacks.description: Higher-level than musical_signature entries — these are named techniques (Iwasawa theory, modular lifting, ergodic methods, etc.). Each known_attack entry references where the technique was documented and which hardness dimension it addresses.

candidate_representations.title: Untested or partially-tested coordinate systems for this problem
candidate_representations.description: Distinct from representation_shift_witnesses (which records shifts that WORKED). These are speculative or in-progress representation candidates. Each: candidate_name, sketch_description, status_marker.

arena_readiness_flags.title: Per-flag readiness assertions for the Arena pipeline
arena_readiness_flags.description: Tracks whether the problem_card has the supporting context the Arena's team-of-three rounds expect. Each flag is set independently; all-true means card is fully Arena-ready.

ready_for_team_round.description: Aggregate: true iff above flags are all true AND no out-of-scope content.

domain_tags.title: Mathematical domain tags
domain_tags.description: Free-form short tags for indexing (e.g. 'number_theory', 'tensor_complexity', 'topological_combinatorics'). Multiple tags allowed.

field_invariants_used.title: Mathematical invariants the classification depends on
field_invariants_used.description: Per Aporia adjudication 2026-05-15 Ask 4 STRONG YES: captures HARD-5 discipline at the per-card level. Records which specific invariants the problem's hardness_signature / known_attacks / method_gap_patterns implicitly depend on. Without this field, the substrate cannot detect cards whose claims silently conflate distinct invariants (e.g. tensor rank R vs border rank R-bar vs asymptotic rank). Each entry names the invariant + optionally cross-references a Tier-B witness primitive in aporia/doctrine/substrate_vocabulary/primitives.md.

hardnessDimensionEnum.title: 8-dimension hardness taxonomy enum
hardnessDimensionEnum.description: Per vision doc Part 4 §The 8-dimension hardness taxonomy. Each dimension names a distinct compression-failure mode. A problem's hardness_signature distributes weight over these.

hardnessSignature.title: Weighted hardness signature over the 8 dimensions
hardnessSignature.description: Required: each of the 8 dimensions present with weight + confidence + citations. Weights need not sum to 1 (a problem can score high on multiple dimensions). Confidence per dimension reflects how confident the classifier is in the weight assignment.

weight.description: Strength of this hardness dimension's contribution to the problem's overall difficulty (0 = irrelevant, 1 = dominant).

confidence.description: Classifier confidence in this weight assignment (0 = wild guess, 1 = certain).

citations.description: Free-form citations supporting the weight+confidence assignment. Use for source attribution; does NOT need to be arXiv/DOI strict.

rationale.description: Brief justification for the weight+confidence combo.

methodGapPattern.title: Method gap pattern (one per known near-miss)

pattern_summary.description: Brief: 'we can prove X with exponent 0.51 but need 0.5'.

closed_theorem.description: Theorem statement that became provable after the shift.

precursor_already_exists.description: False means the precursor itself is a conceptual_absence — chain through to that entry.

tentative_name.description: Working name for the absent object. May change at invention time.

expected_role.description: What this absent object would do if it existed.

nearest_existing_analog.description: Existing structure most-resembling the absent object (e.g. 'like sheaves but with extra symmetry').

absence_confidence.description: How confident we are the object truly doesn't exist (vs. exists-but-unknown).

fieldInvariantEntry.title: One mathematical invariant the card depends on
fieldInvariantEntry.description: Per Aporia 2026-05-15 adjudication Ask 4: machine-queryable record of which invariant a card's claims rest on. Disambiguates close-but-distinct invariants (R vs R-bar; Krull dimension vs polynomial degree; tensor rank vs slice rank vs cactus rank). schema_ref optional but strongly recommended when a Tier-B witness primitive exists in primitives.md.

invariant_name.title: Canonical name of the invariant
invariant_name.description: Short identifier. UpperCamelCase recommended (matches primitiveName convention) but free-form short string accepted.

schema_ref.title: Optional cross-reference to a Tier-B witness primitive
schema_ref.description: Names a primitive in aporia/doctrine/substrate_vocabulary/primitives.md when one exists (TensorRankWitness, BorderRankWitness, AsymptoticSpectrumMonotone, etc.). Empty/absent when no canonical witness primitive is registered yet.

notes.title: Free-form clarification
notes.description: Why this invariant matters for the card; what disambiguation the substrate is preserving by naming it explicitly.

examples.statement: Determine the asymptotic value of the matrix multiplication exponent ω. The trivial lower bound is ω >= 2 (read all n^2 entries); the current upper bound is ω < 2.371339 (Alman-Duan-VW-Xu-Xu-Zhou 2024). It is conjectured but unproven that ω = 2 exactly.

method_gap.rationale: Decade-by-decade improvements via laser method refinements; each step closes constant-factor gaps but doesn't break the structural barrier between 2.37 and 2.

representation_gap.rationale: Group-theoretic embedding (Cohn-Umans) suggested representation may matter; no embedding has yielded ω=2.

exactness_barrier.rationale: Asymptotic evidence is insufficient — need exact bound.

conceptual_absence.rationale: Conjectured: a yet-undiscovered tensor or auxiliary structure with extreme subrank-to-rank ratio would close the gap.

method_gap_patterns.pattern_summary: Laser method on Coppersmith-Winograd auxiliary tensors closes constant-factor gaps but each variant approaches the same limiting exponent ~2.37; structural barrier prevents reaching 2 via this family.

field_invariants_used.notes: Card's hardness signature presumes ordinary (exact) tensor rank R, not border rank R-bar. Substrate must reject any agent attempt to substitute border-rank arguments for tensor-rank arguments here without an explicit theorem bridging the two.
Asymptotic complexity exponent omega is governed by asymptotic rank of M<n>, distinct from finite-n exact rank — relevant to method_gap_pattern boundary.

examples.source_report: Vision doc Part 4 worked example; references AA-013 + T#1.
examples.notes: Worked example for the v0 schema review. Real PC-001 would be filed separately. v0.2.0 update: field_invariants_used populated per Aporia adjudication 2026-05-15 Ask 4 STRONG YES.
